examples: I don't like him, and it would be dishonest of me to pretend otherwise.
In all fairness he had to admit that she was neither dishonest nor lazy.
It would be dishonest to mislead people and not to present the data as fairly as possible.
This is not (merely) dishonest rhetoric designed to fool Westerners, although it has that result.
Their very success demonstrated that public projects were not necessarily inefficient, extravagant and dishonest.
